reasons: Specialty chemicals sector benefits from the China+1 sourcing shift, with global multinationals diversifying their supply chains to Indian manufacturers. The sector has delivered 25%+ earnings CAGR over three years for leading players.

Technically: RSI at 29 signals the stock has been oversold — sellers are exhausted and historically price has mean-reverted strongly from these levels; stock has corrected 17.2% over the past month, creating a meaningful discount to its recent range; price is at the extreme lower end of its volatility range, compounding the oversold signal.
Market backdrop: US markets closed up 0.8% overnight, setting a supportive global tone.
Metals under pressure from China demand uncertainty and rising domestic overcapacity risks. Falling commodity prices globally are squeezing realisation per tonne, directly impacting EBITDA per unit.
Trades at a significant premium to sector peers on P/E with earnings momentum slowing — a re-rating lower looks increasingly likely.
Technically: MACD histogram has crossed into negative territory, confirming momentum has rolled over.
Market backdrop: FII flows are net negative, adding selling pressure across the broader market; India VIX has spiked to 21.1, indicating elevated fear — headwind for risk assets.

Auto sector riding a confluence of rural demand recovery (backed by good monsoon and farm income), a premiumisation wave in passenger vehicles, and early EV adoption tailwinds. The sector has compounded earnings at double-digit rates for the past three years.
Business fundamentals are strong — earnings up 47% and revenue up 26% year-on-year, showing the growth story is backed by real numbers.
